name: Tick Fever (General Tick-Borne Disease)
description: General term for tick-borne febrile illness including Anaplasmosis, mixed Babesia/Theileria infections. Common during tick season in tropical and subtropical regions.
vision_markers: ticks visible on skin especially ears axilla groin

diagnostic_criteria.definitive: Blood smear for tick-borne parasites. Tick count.

treatment_info.veterinary_treatment: Antibiotics for Anaplasmosis (oxytetracycline). Anti-protozoals for Babesia/Theileria. Vet diagnosis required to select correct drug.
supportive_care: Remove ticks manually with tick removers
Apply acaricide (tick spray/dip) under vet guidance
treatment_info.first_aid: Remove visible ticks. Move to cool shade. Provide fresh water. Call vet.
treatment_info.do_not: Do not crush ticks – can release infected material. Do not use same acaricide repeatedly.
